## Table 1: Monthly_highest_temperature_in_Amherst (140 rows × 3 columns)

### Description
Data loaded from CSV file: 461.csv (Dataset: Monthly highest temperature in Amherst)

### Schema (3 fields)
  - Year -- type: int64, values: 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, ..., 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015
  - Date -- type: object, values: 2004/1/1, 2004/10/1, 2004/11/1, 2004/12/1, 2004/2/1, ..., 2015/4/1, 2015/5/1, 2015/6/1, 2015/7/1, 2015/8/1
  - Temperature -- type: int64, values: 41, 42, 44, 47, 49, ..., 94, 95, 96, 97, 98

### Sample Data (first 5 rows)
```
   Year      Date  Temperature
0  2004  2004/1/1           49
1  2004  2004/2/1           54
2  2004  2004/3/1           89
3  2004  2004/4/1           88
4  2004  2004/5/1           92
```

questions: What overall trend in highest monthly temperatures can be observed in Amherst from year to year?
Are there any months where the highest recorded temperature significantly deviates from patterns observed in adjacent months or years?
How do the highest monthly temperatures vary across different years for the same month?
Which years experienced the most extreme monthly high temperatures, and during which months did these occur?
How does the highest monthly temperature change across different years?
Are there any months that consistently show unusually high or low temperatures compared to others?
What patterns of seasonality can be observed in temperature extremes when comparing the same month across multiple years?
Can you identify any years or months in which the highest temperature appears to be an outlier relative to other entries?
